experience.title: Assistant to the Executive Director
experience.description: Supported executive leadership of a community-based organization serving Somali families in the Seattle area, coordinating organizational operations and driving community safety initiatives.
highlights: Coordinated organizational operations and stakeholder communications at the executive level
Supported program planning, grant reporting, and donor relations
Facilitated community engagement and outreach initiatives serving Somali families
Managed scheduling, correspondence, and administrative systems for the Executive Director

experience.title: Resettlement Caseworker Intern
experience.description: Provided direct services to newly arrived refugees navigating the U.S. resettlement process, managing cases and coordinating with partner agencies.
highlights: Managed client cases using IRC case management systems (IRS, GetCare)
Coordinated with partner agencies for service delivery and benefits enrollment
Supported family orientation, documentation, and initial resettlement planning
Conducted home visits and client follow-ups to ensure service continuity

experience.title: Student Engagement & Enrollment Specialist
experience.description: Drove enrollment and student success initiatives for a graduate-level international development program, managing the full recruitment pipeline from prospect to matriculation.
highlights: Managed full enrollment pipeline from initial inquiry through matriculation
Designed and executed outreach campaigns targeting international and domestic applicants
Built and maintained CRM records in Slate to track engagement and optimize conversions
Organized information sessions, campus visits, and virtual events for prospective students
Collaborated with faculty and admissions to improve yield rates

experience.title: Graduate Research Assistant
experience.description: Conducted mixed-methods research on immigration policy and its community impacts, contributing to policy briefs and academic outputs.
highlights: Designed and administered survey instruments for immigration policy research
Coded qualitative interview data using Dedoose for thematic analysis
Contributed to policy briefs and academic publications on immigration topics
Conducted literature reviews and synthesized findings for research reports

experience.title: Nathanson Fellow & GYON Intern
experience.description: Prestigious fellowship focused on youth-led advocacy and international development practice through the Global Youth Opportunity Network (GYON).
highlights: Developed policy analysis products and briefing materials for senior leadership
Participated in the Global Young Officials Network (GYON) international convening
Contributed to cross-sector knowledge exchange on development finance and youth advocacy
Represented the Nathanson Fellows cohort in policy roundtables and convenings

experience.title: Humanitarian Officer — Rohingya & COVID-19 Response
experience.description: Led humanitarian communications and information programming for one of the world's largest displacement crises — the Rohingya refugee response in Cox's Bazar, Bangladesh.
highlights: Designed and implemented information campaigns reaching 100,000+ Rohingya refugees
Coordinated with UNHCR, IOM, and NGO partners on joint information and messaging strategies
Managed community feedback mechanisms and accountability to affected populations (AAP)
Led COVID-19 communications response, adapting programming during pandemic onset
Produced situation reports and briefing notes for humanitarian coordination clusters

experience.title: Field Coordinator
experience.description: Led field data collection and coordination for humanitarian needs assessments in Rohingya refugee camps, managing enumerator teams and ensuring data quality.
highlights: Managed teams of enumerators conducting large-scale household surveys in refugee camps
Oversaw data quality assurance and validation for multi-sector needs assessments
Produced situation reports and briefing notes for humanitarian clusters (ISCG)
Coordinated with camp management agencies and sector leads for assessment access

experience.title: Research Officer — Rohingya Response & Development Sector
experience.description: Conducted program research for BRAC — the world's largest NGO — focused on Rohingya response and social protection and livelihood development programs.
highlights: Designed qualitative and quantitative research protocols for program evaluation
Facilitated focus group discussions with program participants across communities
Analyzed field data and contributed to internal research reports and publications
Supported monitoring and evaluation activities for livelihood and social protection programs
